members.name: Dr. Dimitris Theodoropoulos
members.cv: Dr. Dimitris Theodoropoulos (male) is a senior researcher at the Institute of Communication and Computation Systems (ICCS) of the National Technical University of Athens, and the Microprocessor Hardware Laboratory (MHL) of the Technical University of Crete. In 2003 and 2005 he obtained his Diploma (5-year degree) and M.Sc degree respectively from the Electronic and Computer Engineering department at the Technical University of Crete, Greece. In 2007, he joined the Computer Engineering department of the Delft University of Technology, the Netherlands, and in 2011 he was awarded his Ph.D. Between 2011-2019, he collaborated with the Telecommunication Systems Research Institute at the Technical University of Crete, Greece, and the Computer Architecture and VLSI Systems group at the Foundation for Research and Technology – Hellas (FORTH) in Crete, Greece. Dimitris Theodoropoulos has been involved at technical and coordination level in the following European research and management projects: OPTIMA (#955739), EUROCC (#951732), EUROCC2 (#101101903) EuroHPC JU, EDRA (#851631) H2020 FET Innovation Launchpad, two Tetramax-funded projects (#761349), dReDBox H2020 project (#687632), AXIOM H2020 project (#645496), DeSyRe FP7 project (#287611). He has also coordinated the following nationally (in Greece) founded projects: Eleon (#02637) and mCluster (#1592) supported by the National Strategic Reference Framework (NSRF) 2014-2020. His current research interests include embedded systems, reconfigurable computing, computer architecture, and IoT / cloud computing.

members.name: Andreas Brokalakis
members.cv: MEng (integrated master) from the Computer Engineering and Informatics Dept, University of Patras, Greece. MSc at Intergrated Software / Hardware Systems, from the same Department / University. Currently working towards a PhD degree at the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Technical University of Crete, Greece. I have been working as a research associate for the Telecommunication Systems Institute, Technical University of Crete, engaged in multiple EU and Greek research projects, serving both a researcher/engineer and as project manager. In the past, I have also provided research and development services to Synelixis Solutions Ltd as a senior hardware engineer while currently I hold a similar position for Exascale Performance Systems Ltd. I serve as the Technical and Scientific Manager for the H2020 MEDIATE project and I am also the leader for the integration workpackage in the KDT JU REBECCA project. My expertise lies mostly in the development of reconfigurable systems, computer architecture and computer arithmetic. As part of my involvement in several related projects, I am also rapidly engaged in designing systems related to cybersecurity or systems that provide security mechanisms such as cryptographic systems, homomorphic encryption, zero knwoledge proofs, etc. Lastly, I am responsible for the administration of the server infrastructure of MHL lab.
